# AI BuildOps Tenant Overlay Kit

## Summary
Maps the reusable AI BuildOps pack into a GitHub, Linear, Slack, and Sentry operating stack for pilot and first-production use.

## Extracted Preview
> Ruh.ai V1 Tenant Overlay Kit
> AI BuildOps - GitHub + Linear + Slack + Sentry
> Deployment-ready overlay for one software organization, one trust boundary, and an AI-product delivery conveyor
> 
> VERSION 0.1.0
> PACK REF ai-buildops@1.0.0
> PRIMARY USE Pilot + first production overlay
> DATE 2026-03-08
> 
> Executive snapshot This overlay kit binds the reusable AI BuildOps pack to a delivery stack where GitHub is the code and pull-request system of record, Linear is the planning system of record, Slack is the operator and approval surface, and Sentry is the normalized source for production incidents and regressions. The overlay is designed for teams building AI products that already have modern engineering tools but still suffer from fragmented intake, incomplete specifications, inconsistent handoffs, manual release preparation, and slow incident learning loops. The recommended first activation slice is intake-to-spec, spec-to-build orchestration, eval and release prep, and incident-to-regression learning.
> 
> Figure 1. The overlay binds one reusable delivery pack to one engineering stack and one trust boundary.
> 1. Purpose, scope, and design choices
> This document instantiates the AI BuildOps reference pack as a concrete tenant overlay. It specifies how canonical delivery objects map to GitHub, Linear, Slack, and Sentry; which employees are activated; what permissions and approvals apply; what rollout order is recommended; and how a design-partner deployment should be judged before production promotion.
> Keep GitHub as the source of truth for repositories, branches, pull requests, CI status,...
